name: fhirpathpy

metadata.name: fhirpathpy
classifiers: Topic :: Software Development :: Libraries :: Python Modules

documentation:
fhirpath.py
===========

[![Build Status](https://github.com/beda-software/fhirpath-py/actions/workflows/build.yaml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/beda-software/fhirpath-py/actions)
[![codecov](https://codecov.io/gh/beda-software/fhirpath-py/branch/master/graph/badge.svg)](https://codecov.io/gh/beda-software/fhirpath-py)
[![pypi](https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/fhirpathpy.svg)](https://pypi.org/project/fhirpathpy/)
[![Supported Python version](https://img.shields.io/badge/python-3.8+-blue.svg)](https://www.python.org/downloads/release/python-380/)

[FHIRPath](https://www.hl7.org/fhir/fhirpath.html) implementation in Python

Parser was generated with [antlr4](https://github.com/antlr/antlr4)

# Getting started
## Install
`pip install fhirpathpy`

## Usage
```Python
from fhirpathpy import evaluate

patient = {
  "resourceType": "Patient",
  "id": "example",
  "name": [
    {
      "use": "official",
      "given": [
        "Peter",
        "James"
      ],
      "family": "Chalmers"
    },
    {
      "use": "usual",
      "given": [
        "Jim"
      ]
    },
    {
      "use": "maiden",
      "given": [
        "Peter",
        "James"
      ],
      "family": "Windsor",
      "period": {
        "end": "2002"
      }
    }
  ]
}

# Evaluating FHIRPath
result = evaluate(patient, "Patient.name.where(use='usual').given.first()", [])
# result: `['Jim']`
```

## evaluate
 > Evaluates the "path" FHIRPath expression on the given resource, using data
    from "context" for variables mentioned in the "path" expression.
    Parameters:
    resource (dict|list): FHIR resource, bundle as js object or array of resources This resource will be modified by this function to add type information.
    path (string): fhirpath expression, sample 'Patient.name.given'
    context (dict): a hash of variable name/value pairs.
    model (dict): The "model" data object specific to a domain, e.g. R4.
    Returns:
    int: Description of return value

## compile
> Returns a function that takes a resource and an optional context hash (see
    "evaluate"), and returns the result of evaluating the given FHIRPath
    expression on that resource.  The advantage of this function over "evaluate"
    is that if you have multiple resources, the given FHIRPath expression will
    only be parsed once.
    Parameters:
    path (string) - the FHIRPath expression to be parsed.
    model (dict) - The "model" data object specific to a domain, e.g. R4.

documentation: This file holds code to hande the FHIRPath Math functions.

functions.name: fhirpathpy.engine.invocations.filtering.single_fn
documentation: TODO: behavior on object?

classes.name: fhirpathpy.engine.invocations.constants.SystemTime
documentation:
System date/time should not change during an evaluation of a FHIRPath
expression. It remains the same for the entire expression evaluation.

classes.name: fhirpathpy.engine.invocations.constants.Constants
documentation:
These are values that should not change during an evaluation of a FHIRPath
expression (e.g. the return value of today(), per the spec.)  They are
constant during at least one evaluation.

functions.name: fhirpathpy.engine.invocations.strings.matches
documentation: test function

classes.name: fhirpathpy.engine.nodes.FP_Type

methods.name: fhirpathpy.engine.nodes.FP_Type.equals
documentation:
Tests whether this object is equal to another.  Returns either True,
false, or undefined (where in the FHIRPath specification empty would be
returned).  The undefined return value indicates that the values were the
same to the shared precision, but that they had differnent levels of
precision.

methods.name: fhirpathpy.engine.nodes.FP_Type.equivalentTo
documentation:
Tests whether this object is equivalant to another.  Returns either True,
false, or undefined (where in the FHIRPath specification empty would be
returned).
Class FP_Type is the superclass for FHIRPath types that required special handling

classes.name: fhirpathpy.engine.nodes.FP_Quantity

class_variables.name: fhirpathpy.engine.nodes.FP_Quantity.mapUCUMCodeToTimeUnits
documentation: A map of the UCUM units that must be paired with integer values when doing arithmetic.
A map of the UCUM units that must be paired with integer values when doing arithmetic.

methods.name: fhirpathpy.engine.nodes.FP_TimeBase.equals
documentation:
From the 2020 August:
    For DateTime and Time equality, the comparison is performed by
    considering each precision in order, beginning with years (or hours for
    time values), and respecting timezone offsets. If the values are the
    same, comparison proceeds to the next precision; if the values are
    different, the comparison stops and the result is false. If one input has
    a value for the precision and the other does not, the comparison stops
    and the result is empty ({ }); if neither input has a value for the
    precision, or the last precision has been reached, the comparison stops
    and the result is true.
    Note:  Per the spec above
:return:
    2012-01 = 2012 returns empty
    2012-01 = 2011 returns false
    2012-01 ~ 2012 returns false

---
return: If self.timeMatchData returns DateTime object converted to seconds int, else
  returns None
---

classes.name: fhirpathpy.engine.nodes.ResourceNode

methods.name: fhirpathpy.engine.nodes.ResourceNode.__init__
documentation:
If data is a resource (maybe a contained resource) reset the path
information to the resource type.

*  Constructs a instance for the given node ("data") of a resource.  If the
*  data is the top-level node of a resouce, the path and type parameters will
*  be ignored in favor of the resource's resourceType field.
* @param data the node's data or value (which might be an object with
*  sub-nodes, an array, or FHIR data type)
* @param path the node's path in the resource (e.g. Patient.name).  If the
*  data's type can be determined from data, that will take precedence over
*  this parameter.
* @param _data additional data stored in a property named with "_" prepended.

functions.name: fhirpathpy.engine.invocations.misc.to_date_time
documentation: Defines a function on engine called to+timeType (e.g., toDateTime, etc.).
@param timeType The string name of a class for a time type (e.g. "FP_DateTime").

functions.name: fhirpathpy.evaluate
documentation:
Evaluates the "path" FHIRPath expression on the given resource, using data
from "context" for variables mentioned in the "path" expression.

Parameters:
resource (dict|list): FHIR resource, bundle as js object or array of resources This resource will be modified by this function to add type information.
path (string): fhirpath expression, sample 'Patient.name.given'
context (dict): a hash of variable name/value pairs.
model (dict): The "model" data object specific to a domain, e.g. R4.

Returns:
int: Description of return value

functions.name: fhirpathpy.compile
documentation:
Returns a function that takes a resource and an optional context hash (see
"evaluate"), and returns the result of evaluating the given FHIRPath
expression on that resource.  The advantage of this function over "evaluate"
is that if you have multiple resources, the given FHIRPath expression will
only be parsed once.

Parameters:
path (string) - the FHIRPath expression to be parsed.
model (dict) - The "model" data object specific to a domain, e.g. R4.

For example, you could pass in the result of require("fhirpath/fhir-context/r4")

functions.name: fhirpathpy.engine.invocations.math.plus
documentation: HACK: for only polymorphic function
Actually, "minus" is now also polymorphic

functions.name: fhirpathpy.engine.invocations.equality.remove_duplicate_extension
documentation:
This is a temporary solution for cases where the list contains 2 items with the same key,
like birthDate and _birthDate. Needs to be fixed to a better solution.

functions.name: fhirpathpy.engine.invocations.equality.typecheck
documentation:
Checks that the types of a and b are suitable for comparison in an
inequality expression.  It is assumed that a check has already been made
that there is at least one value in a and b.

Parameters:
a (list) - the left side of the inequality expression (which should be an array of one value)
b (list) -  the right side of the inequality expression (which should be an array of one value)

returns the singleton values of the arrays a, and b.  If one was an FP_Type and the other was convertible, the coverted value will be retureed
